title: Verify the assembled object is a closed boundaryless manifold with π₁ = Γ and simply-connected cover

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: After the equivariant resolution (see the resolution issue), one must verify that $M:=\widetilde M/\Gamma$ is a genuine **closed manifold without boundary**, that $\pi_1(M)\cong\Gamma$ exactly (van Kampen across the resolved strata), and that $\widetilde M$ is simply connected (hence the universal cover). Boundary-freeness and the $\pi_1$ computation are nontrivial because the resolution alters local topology near singular strata.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved: verified that the assembled object is a closed boundaryless manifold with $\pi_1=\Gamma$ and a simply-connected (universal) cover.**

I rewrote the "Assembly of $\widetilde M$ and $M$" subsection (Proposition `prop:hatM` and Theorem `thm:main`) to make the three required verifications rigorous and self-contained. Concretely:

**1. Closed, boundaryless smooth manifold.** Previously the closedness was asserted in one line. I now give a full proof:
- Fix $F$-equivariant collars $\kappa_F,\kappa_F'$ of the common boundary sphere bundle $S(\nu)$ in the exterior $E$ and in each resolved tube $R_F$; the radial collapse $r_F$ is the identity on a collar, so it matches the identity on $E$ across the seam.
- A seam point lies in exactly one $S(\nu)$ (deepest-first processing keeps distinct frontiers disjoint closed manifolds, since lower strata where they could touch are already replaced by free pieces). Hence exactly two pieces meet there, and the explicit collar chart $(x,t)\mapsto \kappa_F(x,t)$ ($t\ge0$) / $\kappa_F'(x,-t)$ ($t\le0$) gives a **boundaryless** smooth chart $\cong S(\nu)\times(-1,1)$. So $\partial\widehat M_0=\varnothing$: every boundary component of $E$ is matched to exactly one tube boundary, no free boundary survives. Compactness is a finite union of compacta.
- $M=\widehat M_0/Q$ is then closed and boundaryless because $Q$ acts freely (properly discontinuously) and the quotient of a boundaryless manifold by a free finite action has no boundary.

**2. $\pi_1(M)\cong\Gamma$ (van Kampen across resolved strata).** I made the comparison genuinely rigorous rather than the previous informal "two amalgamated products coincide":
- Connectivity facts established: $E$, $\mathcal O$, and each link $L_F=S(\nu)/F$ are connected (slice codim $m\ge3$).
- Key clean fact (eq. `linkgroup`): since $S(V)=S^{m-1}$ is simply connected for $m\ge3$, the fiber link group is exactly $F$, and $S(\nu)\to L_F$ is the relevant cover with monodromy $\pi_1(L_F)\twoheadrightarrow F$.
- Both $\pi_1(M)$ (eq. `vk-M`) and $\Gamma=\pi_1^{orb}(\mathcal O)$ (eq. `vk-orb`) are **pushouts of the same diagram** $\pi_1(\mathcal O^{reg})\leftarrow \pi_1(L_F)\rightarrow G_F$ with the identical left leg; the only difference is the right factor $G_F=\pi_1(V_F)$ vs. $G_F=F$.
- By (M3)/`lem:simpconn`, $\pi_1(V_F)\cong F$, and crucially this isomorphism **intertwines the amalgamating maps**: both right legs equal the covering monodromy of $S(\nu)\to L_F$. An isomorphism of one factor intertwining the amalgamating maps induces an isomorphism of pushouts (functoriality of the colimit), applied at all strata simultaneously $\Rightarrow \pi_1(M)\cong\Gamma$, induced by the resolution/classifying map.

**3. Simply-connected (universal) cover.** Made explicit: $M$ is connected, has a universal cover $\widetilde M$ with $\pi_1(\widetilde M)=1$ and deck group $\pi_1(M)\cong\Gamma$, acting freely/properly/cocompactly with $\widetilde M/\Gamma=M$. Equivalently the cover associated to $\ker(\pi_1(M)\xrightarrow{\cong}\Gamma)=1$ is the universal cover, so $\widetilde M$ is genuinely simply connected — not merely "a $\Gamma$-cover."

Updated the Summary item 5 accordingly. The new text balances ($\verb|\begin/\end|$, braces, all `\ref` resolve); no LaTeX toolchain available to compile.

**Caveat (honest scope, unchanged):** these verifications are unconditional in the $F=\mathbb{Z}/2$ case (links $=\mathbb{RP}^{m-1}$, $m$ even). For general finite $2$-group stabilizers they rest on the existence of the local model $V_F$ with $\partial V_F=$ resolved link on the nose, i.e. the integral null-bordance hypothesis isolated in §`sec:induct` (rationally always true; the $N$-fold rational version suffices for the rational conclusion). That bordism input is the subject of the separate resolution issue and is not re-litigated here; the manifold/$\pi_1$/cover verifications in this issue are valid given a model satisfying (M1)–(M3) with the stated boundary.
